questions.question:
Which of the following statements is FALSE?



Theory of Computation: TIFR CSE 2013 | Part B | Question: 11
options: The intersection of a context free language with a regular language is context free.
The intersection of two regular languages is regular.
The intersection of two context free languages is context free
The intersection of a context free language and the complement of a regular language is context free.
The intersection of a regular language and the complement of a regular language is regular.
questions.correct_answer: The intersection of two context free languages is context free

questions.question:
Which the following is FALSE?



Theory of Computation: TIFR CSE 2014 | Part B | Question: 14
options: Complement of a recursive language is recursive.
A language recognized by a non-deterministic Turing machine can also be recognized by a deterministic Turing machine.
Complement of a context free language can be recognized by a Turing machine.
If a language and its complement are both recursively enumerable then it is recursive.
Complement of a non-recursive language can never be recognized by any Turing machine.

questions.question:
Which language class has the following properties?

\( \quad \) It is closed under union and intersection but not complement.



Theory of Computation: TIFR CSE 2016 | Part B | Question: 2
